name: Sphinx
oneliner: Sphinx deploys browser-native AI agents that operate directly inside existing compliance systems — case management platforms, third-party portals, email, and PDFs — to automate AML, KYC, and KYB investigations without requiring institutions to adopt new tooling.
finserv: Purpose-built for financial crime compliance; millions of alerts and hundreds of thousands of cases handled in production; Equals Money reported 94% reduction in false positives; multiple internationally-operating financial institutions as customers; backed by Y Combinator and Cherry Ventures
team: Co-founded by Alexandre Berkovic and Chrisjan Wüst. Backed by Cherry Ventures (lead), Y Combinator, Rebel Fund, Deel Ventures, and Singularity Capital. $7.1M seed round closed February 2026.
thesis: This is a near-direct fit for the compliance layer of your regulated infrastructure thesis. Sphinx operates at the workflow level inside the tools institutions already use, which positions it as a potential module in a broader compliance-grade agent stack rather than a competing platform. The browser-native, no-new-system architecture removes the primary adoption barrier in compliance-heavy institutions.
